pairs.question: What was the start and end date of the 2009 Kerrick Sports Sedan Series?
pairs.answer: The 2009 Kerrick Sports Sedan Series began on 25 April 2009 at Wakefield Park and ended on 22 November at Phillip Island Grand Prix Circuit.

pairs.question: How many rounds and races were there in the 2009 Kerrick Sports Sedan Series?
pairs.answer: The 2009 Kerrick Sports Sedan Series consisted of five rounds and a total of fifteen races.

pairs.question: Which types of automobiles were eligible to compete in the 2009 Kerrick Sports Sedan Series?
pairs.answer: The series was open to Group 3D Sports Sedans, Tran-Am automobiles, and New Zealand TraNZam automobiles.

pairs.question: Who won the 2009 Kerrick Sports Sedan Series, and how many races did they win?
pairs.answer: Des Wall won the 2009 Kerrick Sports Sedan Series. He won three races during the season.

pairs.question: How did Des Wall secure the series victory despite winning fewer races than Tony Ricciardello and Darren Hossack?
pairs.answer: Des Wall secured the series victory by consistently finishing in the top five, with only one race outside the top five (an eighth-place finish at Oran Park). This consistency allowed him to overcome Tony Ricciardello and Darren Hossack, who each had more race wins but also more mechanical unreliability and non-finishes.

pairs.question: Can you calculate the point difference between the top three drivers in the 2009 Kerrick Sports Sedan Series?
pairs.answer: Des Wall won by twelve points over Tony Ricciardello, while Darren Hossack finished three points behind Ricciardello. The point differences can be calculated as follows: Wall (1st) - Ricciardello (2nd) = 12 points; Ricciardello (2nd) - Hossack (3rd) = 3 points.

pairs.question: Who was the only driver besides Des Wall, Tony Ricciardello, and Darren Hossack to win a race in the 2009 series, and in which race did this occur?
pairs.answer: Chris Jackson was the only other driver to win a race in the 2009 series. He won at Oran Park in his Opel Calibra.
